externalPage.customer_compat_text: The live preview here renders content inside a sandboxed iframe, so full HTML documents, <script> blocks and embedded YouTube/Vimeo videos all work. However, the external page customers see is a separate app — for these features to also display on the customer side, the same iframe approach must be used there. Otherwise the page may appear blank or unstyled. A simple HTML snippet is the safest option for cross-app compatibility.

editRestaurant.title: Edit Restaurant — Help
editRestaurant.intro: Edit ALL basic restaurant information here: the name customers see in the menu, contact info, full street address, map location, and menu images. These are the foundation; they don't change often, but if they're wrong customers can't find you.

sections.heading: Restaurant Name
sections.body: The first name customers see when opening the menu. Should match your brand — keep it consistent with social media, invoices, signage. Auto-formatted to Title Case as you type.

sections.heading: Phone
sections.body: How customers reach you and how the system contacts you about online orders. Country code is automatic (+90 for TR); you only enter the 10-digit local number. Wrong number = harder order confirmation.
sections.example: 5XX XXX XX XX (10 digits, no leading 0)
sections.warning: Use a line you actually monitor — most negative-feedback calls go to this number.

sections.heading: City
sections.body: City the restaurant is in. Auto-formatted to title case.

sections.heading: District
sections.body: District the restaurant is in. Combined with city + neighbourhood for the 'full address'.

sections.heading: Neighbourhood
sections.body: Neighbourhood the restaurant is in.

sections.heading: Street Address
sections.body: Avenue / street / building / floor / apt detail. Shows on invoices and customer directions. Don't repeat city/district/neighbourhood — those are above already.

sections.heading: Location (Map)
sections.body:
The EXACT pin on the map. Just typing the address isn't enough — Google Maps can be 100m off. Click 'Edit Location' and drag the pin precisely onto your building.

Used in two places:
• Online-order distance calculation (5 km rule, etc.)
• Customer turn-by-turn directions from their phone.
sections.warning: Wrong pin = wrong distance = orders either get rejected or accepted from way too far away.

sections.heading: Background Image (Menu Hero)
sections.body:
The big banner at the top of the menu. Your interior, exterior, or a signature dish works well. Landscape orientation looks best.

The system auto-resizes to ~1000px — even if you upload a 4K phone photo, you don't lose quality.
sections.tip: Pick an atmospheric image without text/logo overlay — the menu's typography handles text already.

sections.heading: Logo
sections.body: Your brand logo, shown small in the menu corner. Sits in front of the background image.
sections.tip: Use a JPG and you'll see a square coloured frame around the logo. Transparent PNG always looks cleaner.

sections.heading: Save
sections.body: The indigo button at the bottom right. Sends every change in one go. Leaving the page without saving discards your edits.

settings.title: General Settings — Help
settings.intro: All your restaurant's 'infrastructure' settings: web subdomain, language/currency, which order types you accept, slogans the customer sees, custom price label, and the master 'hide' switch.

sections.heading: Tenant (Subdomain)
sections.body:
Your menu's web address. Comes out as '<tenant>.liwamenu.com' and is encoded inside every QR. The 'Check' button next to the field verifies whether that name is available.

Once saved, the field locks; clicking 'Change' opens a red warning modal that you must confirm before unlocking.
sections.warning: Changing the tenant = every printed QR points to a dead address. Don't change unless you have to.

sections.heading: Menu Language
sections.body: The DEFAULT language. The first thing the customer sees when opening the menu is in this language. Customers can switch from the top right, but the default is this.
sections.example: Pick 'Turkish' for a restaurant in Türkiye.

sections.heading: Currency Symbol
sections.body: Single character or short symbol shown next to prices. The system doesn't auto-write 'TL' — whatever you enter shows up.

sections.heading: Decimal Places
sections.body: Controls how many digits appear after the decimal point in money figures. Most currencies use 2 (e.g. cents); currencies without a fractional unit (e.g. Japanese Yen) can use 0. This only affects how prices are displayed in the customer menu — the prices you enter aren't changed.

sections.heading: Google Analytics
sections.body: Google Analytics measurement ID for tracking who visits your menu, when, and which page. Free account at analytics.google.com — copy the measurement ID.
sections.tip: Optional; leave blank if you don't want it. Data goes straight to Google, not us.

sections.heading: Top Slogan
sections.body: Short line shown at the top of the menu. Use for a welcome message, brand slogan, or campaign announcement.

sections.heading: Bottom Slogan
sections.body: Short note at the bottom of the menu, after the customer has seen all the products. Good for thanks, follow-on call, or sign-off.

sections.heading: Online Order (Delivery)
sections.body:
When on, customers can order remotely (delivery to home/office). Activating unlocks 4 fields below:

• Discount Rate (%) — auto-applied to online orders
• Delivery Fee (₺) — added to order total
• Minimum Order (₺) — orders below this aren't accepted
• Maximum Distance (km) — customers beyond this can't order (REQUIRED)
sections.warning: AT LEAST ONE payment method must be defined before activating. Otherwise the system redirects you to the Payment Methods tab.

sections.heading: In-Person Order (QR Order)
sections.body:
When on, customers can scan the table QR and order directly.

Sub-options:
• Table Discount Rate (%) — applied to table orders
• Table Distance Check — when on, only customers within a metres-radius of the restaurant can order (blocks fake remote orders)
• Maximum Table Distance (m) — radius for the check
sections.example: Active + 50 m check → only customers within 50 m of the restaurant can place a table order.

sections.heading: Special Pricing Active
sections.body: Lets you show some products at an alternate price (member price, sale, campaign). When on, the product edit page shows two price fields.

sections.heading: Special Price Label
sections.body: Appears when Special Pricing Active is on. The label customers see next to the alternate price.

sections.heading: Hide Restaurant
sections.body: Closes your menu to ALL access. Turn on for vacation, renovation, or temporary closure — customers landing on the menu see a 'closed' notice.
sections.example: Annual holiday or renovation period (hide for 1 week)
sections.warning: Don't forget you turned it on — while hidden, no one can access the menu, online orders, or table ordering.

reservationSettings.title: Reservation Settings — Help
reservationSettings.intro: Let customers request a table from the menu and define the rules. Reservation requests appear in the admin panel's 'Reservations' page; you approve or reject them.

sections.heading: Reservations Active
sections.body: When on, a 'Make Reservation' button appears at the top of the menu. When off, the button is hidden and no requests come in.
sections.tip: Leaving it on is convenient for restaurants with weekend crowds — customers book through the system instead of constantly calling.

sections.heading: Start Time
sections.body: Earliest time of day you accept reservations. No slots open before this.

sections.heading: End Time
sections.body: Latest time of day you accept reservations. No slots open after this.
sections.example: Kitchen closes at 23:00 → End: 22:00 (so the last guests can finish)

sections.heading: Interval (minutes)
sections.body: Spacing between reservation slots. Smaller = more options (but harder on staff); larger = fewer slots but easier to manage.

sections.heading: Maximum Party Size
sections.body: Largest single-reservation group size. Bigger groups can't book through the form (you might want to handle weddings/groups by phone).
sections.example: 10 → a 12-person group can't book in one reservation

externalPage.title: External Page — Help
externalPage.intro: Add an extra page that opens behind a button in the customer menu. Pick either custom HTML (Tailwind-supported) or a single long image (e.g. a menu graphic). Each field is explained below.

sections.heading: Page Type (HTML / Image)
sections.body: Two options: HTML Content (custom design with Tailwind classes) or Image (a single image file, typically a long menu graphic). The theme always shows ONLY the selected mode; saving clears the other on the backend.
sections.tip: If you just want to share a long menu graphic, Image mode is the fastest. For a richer card / promo page, use HTML mode.

sections.heading: Button Name (Text)
sections.body: The label your customer sees on the button that opens this page in the main menu. Max 40 characters. REQUIRED whenever you have publishable content (HTML or image) — leaving it blank blocks the save with a toast warning.

sections.heading: HTML Content (Textarea)
sections.body:
Type your page's HTML into the dark panel. Tailwind CSS classes are supported (rounded-xl, bg-amber-50, text-2xl, etc.). You can paste either a full HTML document (...) or just a snippet (
...
). The editor automatically wraps snippets in a Tailwind CDN wrapper.
sections.example:
About Us

Serving Liwa flavors for 15 years...
sections.warning: HTML content cannot be empty while HTML mode is selected. If you only want to stop publishing, switch to Image mode and remove any image too.

sections.heading: Live Preview — Mobile / Desktop Toggle
sections.body: The right panel is the LIVE preview — it updates on every keystroke. Use the Mobile (360px) and Desktop (768px) buttons in the top right to switch widths. The preview runs inside a sandboxed iframe; <script>, embedded YouTube videos and your own <style> tags all render the way real customers will see them.
sections.tip: Most customers open the menu via QR on a phone — always check the Mobile view. Wide embeds like YouTube auto-scale to 100% width with a 16:9 aspect ratio.

sections.heading: Image (Image Mode)
sections.body: Upload a single image file: PNG / JPG / GIF / WebP. Typical use is showing a long menu graphic as-is. The 'Full Preview' button shows the customer's full-screen perspective; 'Remove' clears the uploaded image. Picking a new image opens a crop dialog so you can frame the area you want.
sections.warning: Max file size follows the VITE_MAX_FILE_SIZE_MB setting (default 5 MB). Larger files are rejected.

sections.heading: Security Filter (on save)
sections.body:
On save the HTML is scanned for the patterns below. If any match, the save is blocked and the toast lists exactly which pattern triggered:
• <meta http-equiv="refresh"> (silent redirect)
• <base> (URL hijack)
• javascript: / vbscript: / data:text/html URL schemes
• SQL injection patterns (DROP TABLE, UNION SELECT, OR 1=1, etc.)

Important: <script> and embedded <iframe> are NOT blocked — your legitimate animations and videos are allowed.
sections.tip: The toast names the offending pattern explicitly — remove it and save again.

sections.heading: Customer Menu Compatibility Note
sections.body: The live preview here uses a sandboxed iframe. The external page customers see is a separate app (menu themes). Full HTML documents, <script> blocks and embedded media (YouTube / Vimeo) require the customer side to use the same iframe approach. Otherwise the page may appear blank or unstyled for customers.
sections.tip: For maximum compatibility, prefer simple HTML snippets written with Tailwind classes.

announcementSettings.title: Announcement Settings — Help
announcementSettings.intro: Design a popup announcement shown to every customer when they open the menu. Use for campaigns, grand openings, special days, new menu launches. If you know HTML/Tailwind you have full freedom; if not, the 'Magic Generate' button asks AI for a ready template.

sections.heading: Enable Announcement
sections.body: When on, the announcement appears as a popup in the centre of the menu when a customer opens it. When off, nothing shows.
sections.example: Run a New Year menu announcement for one week, then turn it off.

sections.heading: Display Delay (ms)
sections.body: How many milliseconds AFTER opening the menu the announcement should appear. 0 = instant, 2000 = 2 seconds later. Too fast = customer doesn't read it, too slow = they may not notice.
sections.example: 1500 ms (1.5 sec) — right after the customer's eyes adjust to the menu

sections.heading: HTML Content
sections.body: Left panel = HTML code, right panel = live preview. You can use Tailwind CSS classes (rounded-xl, bg-amber-50, text-2xl, etc.). Not a designer/developer? Don't touch — use 'Magic Generate' instead.
sections.example:
New Year Menu!

Book your table for Dec 31st
sections.tip: Whatever you type in the dark panel renders instantly in the right preview card.

sections.heading: Magic Generate
sections.body: Tell AI what kind of announcement you want (write in any language) and it generates HTML for you. Try 'New Year menu announcement, animated, with star icons'.
sections.example: Prompt: 'Neon-coloured, fun announcement for our 10-year campaign'

sections.heading: Live Preview
sections.body: Right-side card — exactly what the customer sees. While disabled, it blurs with a 'Preview Disabled' label.

sections.heading: Save Changes
sections.body: Indigo button at the bottom right. Leaving without saving discards your changes.

surveySettings.title: Survey Settings — Help
surveySettings.intro: Collect feedback from customers about your service quality. Survey categories (food, service, ambiance, etc.) show at the end of the menu as star ratings, and you track results here.

sections.heading: Add Survey Category (+ Button)
sections.body: The '+ Add Survey' button at the top right opens a new rating category. For each: a title (e.g. 'Service Speed'), an icon (emoji), and an optional description.

sections.heading: Category List
sections.body:
Each row = a category; shows average rating + number of reviews + star count. Colour code lets you read quality at a glance:
• Green ≥ 4.5 (excellent)
• Blue ≥ 4.0 (good)
• Amber < 4.0 (needs improvement)
• Grey = no ratings yet

sections.heading: Edit / Delete
sections.body: Pencil icon on each category row edits content; trash icon deletes. Deletion isn't undoable; ratings on a deleted category don't transfer to a new one you add.

sections.heading: Performance Insight
sections.body: Indigo banner at the bottom of the page. Auto-finds your highest-rated category and shows '⭐ Strongest category: …'. Tells you what customers love most.
sections.tip: If no ratings yet, a Sparkles icon shows 'No feedback yet'.

sections.heading: Overall Counter
sections.body: Below the hero title: 'X categories · Y reviews · ⭐ Z'. Z is the average across all categories.

workingHours.title: Working Hours — Help
workingHours.intro: Define which days of the week and which hours the restaurant is open. Customers can't order during closed hours. The 'Are we open today?' check uses this data.

sections.heading: Day Toggle (Open / Closed)
sections.body:
Each row = one day; the toggle on the left sets that day's open/closed state.

• When ON, you fill in the hours for that day; row background turns indigo.
• When OFF, time fields go faded grey and aren't editable.

The first time you open a day, hours auto-fill to 08:00 – 23:59; tweak as you like.

sections.heading: Open Time
sections.body: Earliest time you accept orders that day. Orders before this aren't accepted.

sections.heading: Close Time
sections.body: Latest time you accept orders. Usually a bit before the kitchen actually closes so the last orders can be prepared.
sections.example: Kitchen closes at 23:00 → Close: 22:30 (30 min prep buffer)

sections.heading: Weekday vs Weekend
sections.body: Different hours per day are fully supported. Late Friday–Saturday, early Sunday close, Monday fully closed — all common.
sections.tip: Restaurants busy on weekends often push the close time 1–2 hours later to give customers an extended window.

sections.heading: Save
sections.body: Indigo button at the bottom right. Leaving without saving discards changes. If you've marked a day open without filling its hours, you'll see a warning.

socialMedias.title: Social Media — Help
socialMedias.intro: Add links to your social profiles. They show as small platform icons at the bottom of the menu. Customers tap them to open your profile in a new tab. Five platforms supported.

sections.heading: Facebook
sections.body: Full URL of your Facebook page. Copy the page/profile address as-is.

sections.heading: Instagram
sections.body: Full URL of your Instagram profile. Just the @handle isn't enough.

sections.heading: TikTok
sections.body: Profile URL for your TikTok account. Includes the '@' character.

sections.heading: YouTube
sections.body: Your YouTube channel address. Starts with /channel/... or /@channelname.

sections.heading: WhatsApp
sections.body: Direct WhatsApp link customers can use to message you. The wa.me/number format works; country code + number, no leading +.
sections.tip: If your restaurant phone is set, it auto-suggests in the placeholder.

sections.heading: Empty Fields
sections.body: Leave platforms you don't have empty. The customer's menu only shows icons for platforms with values; empties don't appear.
sections.example: No TikTok? Leave it blank; the TikTok icon won't show in the menu.

sections.heading: ↗ Side Link Button
sections.body: Arrow icon to the right of each row opens the entered URL in a new tab. Use it to verify the link lands on the right profile.

sections.heading: Save
sections.body: Indigo button at the bottom right. Sends every platform in one go.

paymentMethods.title: Payment Methods — Help
paymentMethods.intro: Pick which methods customers can use during online order checkout. If online ordering is on, AT LEAST ONE method must be active; otherwise the system warns you.

sections.heading: Add Method (+ Button)
sections.body: The '+ Add Method' button at the top right defines a new payment method. Free-form name — 'Cash on Delivery', 'Card on Delivery', 'Online Card', 'Bank Transfer', 'Sodexo', 'Multinet', etc.

sections.heading: Enable All (Master Toggle)
sections.body: Single tap to turn ALL defined methods on/off at once. Quick bulk action.
sections.warning: While Online Order is active, you can't disable everything — the system requires at least one method to stay active.

sections.heading: Per-Method On/Off
sections.body: Toggle on each row turns that method on/off. Customer only sees active methods.
sections.tip: If WhatsApp ordering is common in your area, keeping 'Bank Transfer' active can help.

sections.heading: Delete Method (Trash Icon)
sections.body: Red trash icon on each row removes that method permanently. A confirmation popup appears; deletion isn't undoable.
sections.warning: Past orders that used this method aren't affected, but future orders won't see it as an option.

sections.heading: Counter
sections.body: The hero subtitle shows '3 / 5 active' — total defined / how many are active.

sections.heading: Save
sections.body: Click Save at the bottom right to send your on/off changes. Adding/deleting is instant; on/off toggles need Save to persist.

categories.title: Categories — Help
categories.intro: Split your menu into categories. Each appears as a tab/page in the menu. Use the List tab to edit one by one, or the Bulk tab to upload images for many at once.

sections.heading: Add Category (+ Button)
sections.body: Indigo '+ Add Category' at top right opens a new category popup. Asks for: name (required), image (required), and which menus it belongs to (if any).
sections.example: New: 'Seasonal Salads' + a salad bowl photo

sections.heading: List Tab
sections.body: Lists every category as a card. Each card shows: image + name + product count + edit/delete buttons.
sections.tip: Lots of categories means a long page; don't keep ones you don't need.

sections.heading: Bulk Tab
sections.body: See categories without images and upload them all in a single batch. Perfect for new restaurants — no need to open 10 separate dialogs for 10 categories.
sections.example: 10 categories → bulk mode shows them side by side; assign images and Save once.

sections.heading: Sorting (Drag & Drop)
sections.body: In the List tab, drag the category cards to reorder. The order is saved automatically.
sections.tip: Put best-sellers or items you want to push at the top — that's what customers see first.

sections.heading: Edit (Pencil Icon)
sections.body: Edit the category's name, image, and which menus it belongs to.

sections.heading: Delete (Trash Icon)
sections.body: Permanently removes the category. A confirmation popup appears.
sections.warning: Deleting a category affects its products and subcategories. Move products to another category first.

sections.heading: Product List Indicator
sections.body: The number on each category card = how many products it contains. Click it and that category's products list in a popup.

subCategories.title: Subcategories — Help
subCategories.intro: Split big categories into a second level: Pizza > Meat / Vegetarian / Mixed. Customers entering the parent category see the subcategories as a filter strip and tap one to narrow down faster.

sections.heading: Add Subcategory (+ Button)
sections.body:
The indigo '+ Add Subcategory' button at the top right opens a popup. Asks for:
• Name (e.g. 'Meat Pizzas')
• Image (optional)
• Parent CATEGORY (required)
sections.example: New: 'Meat' subcategory bound to the 'Pizzas' parent category

sections.heading: Binding to a Parent Category
sections.body: Every subcategory belongs to one parent. The list shows parents as headings, with their subcategories underneath.
sections.example:
Pizzas (parent)
  └─ Meat Pizzas
  └─ Vegetarian Pizzas
  └─ Mixed Pizzas
sections.warning: To move a subcategory to a different parent, use the Edit (pencil) button to change the parent. Drag-and-drop only sorts within the same parent.

sections.heading: Drag & Drop Sorting
sections.body: Use the handle icon at the left to grab and drag. Sorting works WITHIN the same parent category; you can't drag a subcategory across parents.

sections.heading: Save Order
sections.body: When you change the order, a 'Save Order' button appears at the top. Until you click it, changes aren't persisted.
sections.tip: Drag several subcategories first, then save once for performance.

sections.heading: Edit / Delete
sections.body: Each subcategory row has pencil (edit) and trash (delete) icons on the right. Delete shows a confirmation popup.
sections.warning: Deleting a subcategory doesn't directly affect its products — they stay in the parent category, just lose the subcategory tag.

sections.heading: Product Count Badge
sections.body: Right of each subcategory: 'X products' badge. How many products are tagged with this subcategory.

sections.heading: Open / Closed Badge
sections.body: Green 'OPEN' = active; muted 'CLOSED' = inactive. Closed subcategories don't show to customers.

products.title: Products — Help
products.intro: The main page for managing every product in your menu. The top tabs switch between Manage / Price List / Add Product. This help covers the Manage tab.

sections.heading: Top Tabs
sections.body:
Three tabs at the very top:
• Manage Products (List) — this page, for viewing/editing every product
• Price List — fast bulk price editing
• Add Product — form to create a new product

sections.heading: Counter (Total Products)
sections.body: The hero subtitle shows 'X products' — the total in your restaurant. Updates as you apply filters.

sections.heading: Search Box
sections.body:
Magnifier-icon box at top left. Filters locally as you type — no need to press Enter.

DIACRITIC-INSENSITIVE: 'i' = 'ı' = 'I' = 'İ', 'o' = 'ö', 'u' = 'ü', 'c' = 'ç', 'g' = 'ğ', 's' = 'ş'.

sections.heading: Clear Search (X)
sections.body: When you've typed in the search box, an 'X' appears on the right; clicking it clears the search.

sections.heading: Category Filter
sections.body:
Dropdown next to the search box. 'All Categories' is the default; pick one and only that category's products show.

The category filter runs server-side → list refreshes, page resets to 1.

sections.heading: Status Filter (Open / Closed / All)
sections.body:
Filter products by sale status:
• All → everything
• Open → in-stock, on sale
• Closed → sold out / inactive

sections.heading: Duplicates Switch
sections.body: On the filter strip, a 'Duplicate products' toggle. Turn on to scan all products for items with the same name. Catches accidentally double-added entries.
sections.warning: The backend caps a page at 100 products; finding duplicates correctly fetches ALL products page by page. Wait a moment if you have many.

sections.heading: Clear Filters
sections.body: When any filter is active (search + category + status + duplicates), a 'Clear' button appears in the strip. One click resets to defaults.

sections.heading: Product Card — Image
sections.body: Square image on the left. If absent, a default placeholder shows. Clicking the card opens the edit page.

sections.heading: Product Card — Name and Description
sections.body: Name (large, bold) and below it the description in small text. Long descriptions show 2 lines and truncate.

sections.heading: Product Card — CLOSED Badge
sections.body: If a product is closed (sold out / inactive), the image corner shows a red 'CLOSED' badge. Open products don't show a badge (no need).

sections.heading: Product Card — Portion Badge
sections.body: If the product has multiple portions (e.g. 'Small/Medium/Large'), the count is shown. Single-portion items don't show this badge.

sections.heading: Product Card — Price
sections.body: Lowest portion's price + currency symbol. The 'starting from' price.

sections.heading: Product Card — Edit / Delete
sections.body: Right side has two icons: pencil (edit) and trash (delete). Delete shows a confirmation popup.
sections.warning: Deleting one product should be under a second; if the backend errors, a toast appears.

sections.heading: Product Card — Bulk Select Checkbox
sections.body: Top-left of every card. Checking turns the card pink-toned and reveals the 'bulk action' bars at top and bottom.
sections.tip: Once one card is selected, a 'Select All' option appears in the header — one click selects every visible product.

sections.heading: Bulk Action Bar
sections.body: When one or more products are selected, an 'X products selected' bar appears at both the top and bottom. The 'Delete' button at the right opens a bulk-delete confirmation.
sections.warning: Bulk delete isn't undoable! The confirmation popup re-shows the count to be deleted.

sections.heading: Pagination
sections.body: Bottom of the page: 'Previous / 1 2 3 ... / Next' buttons. Each page shows 20 products.
sections.tip: If there are more than 5 pages, a 'Go to page' input appears — type a page number and click 'Go' to jump.

sections.heading: Liwa AI (Add/Edit pages)
sections.body: On the Add/Edit Product pages, some fields have a 'Generate with Liwa AI' button. NOT YET ACTIVE — coming soon, currently grey.

menus.title: Menus — Help
menus.intro: A 'Menu' is a bundle of categories that's active on specific days at specific hours. 'Breakfast Menu', 'Dinner Menu', 'Late-Night Menu', etc. Whichever menu is active when the customer opens the app — they see those categories and products.

sections.heading: Top Counter
sections.body: The hero subtitle shows 'X menus' — total menus defined in your restaurant.

sections.heading: Add Menu (+ Button)
sections.body: The indigo '+ Add Menu' button at the top right opens a popup. Asks for the menu name, which categories to include, and time plans (hour range + days).

sections.heading: Menu Card — Title
sections.body: Each card's top: the menu name (e.g. 'Breakfast Menu').

sections.heading: Menu Card — Plan Count Badge
sections.body: Shows how many time plans this menu has. If you have 'weekday morning, weekend midday', the badge says '2 plans'.

sections.heading: Menu Card — Category Count Badge
sections.body: Number of categories assigned. The badge appears when there's at least 1 category; 0 hides it.

sections.heading: Menu Card — Edit (Pencil)
sections.body: Pencil icon on the right of the card. Opens a popup with the menu's name, categories, and time plans; tweak any field and save.

sections.heading: Menu Card — Delete (Trash)
sections.body: Next to the pencil. A confirmation popup appears; deleting the menu doesn't affect categories assigned to it (they remain).

sections.heading: Time Plan List
sections.body:
Each plan is its own row in the card body. Format: '07:00 – 11:00' + days.
• 'Every Day' badge → all 7 days selected
• Otherwise short labels: Mon, Tue, Wed...

sections.heading: Empty State
sections.body: If you haven't created any menus yet, an empty state card with 'No menus yet' appears in the middle — pointing to the '+ Add Menu' button.

sections.heading: Add Menu — Name
sections.body: First field in the popup. The internal name (not shown to customers) — used by you to tell menus apart. Keep it short and clear.

sections.heading: Add Menu — Categories
sections.body: All your restaurant's categories show as a checkbox list. Tick the ones to include in this menu. A category can belong to multiple menus.

sections.heading: Add Menu — Time Plan
sections.body: Add one or more time plans. Each: start time + end time + which days of the week.
sections.example:
Plan 1: 07:00 – 11:00 — Mon, Tue, Wed, Thu, Fri
Plan 2: 09:00 – 13:00 — Sat, Sun

sections.heading: Add Menu — Plan Add/Remove
sections.body: '+ Add Plan' creates a new plan row; trash icon next to each plan removes it. At least one plan is required.

sections.heading: Overlapping Menus
sections.body: If multiple menus are active at the same time, the customer sees the UNION of their categories. To avoid overlap, design your hours so plans don't stack.
sections.tip: Define your main menus (Breakfast / Dinner / Late) first, then plan their hours so they don't overlap.

orderTags.title: Tags — Help
orderTags.intro: A 'Tag Group' = a set of extra options offered to the customer at order time. Doneness, extra toppings, sauce choice, temperature preference. A tag group can bind to many products; when the customer adds that product, the group opens automatically.

sections.heading: Header Counter
sections.body: Hero subtitle shows 'X groups'; if there are unsaved (dirty) groups, a 'Y dirty' badge appears. Saved/clean shows 'X groups ready'.

sections.heading: Save All Changes (Bulk Save)
sections.body: The RED 'Save All Changes' button at top right only appears when at least one group is dirty. Sends every dirty + new group in one dispatch.
sections.warning: The backend assigns new ids to new groups; if you don't refresh the page, local ids may stay stale. After saving, the cache is invalidated and fresh data is fetched on the next visit.

sections.heading: + Add Group
sections.body: Indigo '+ Add Group' at top right adds an empty group card. Doesn't allow adding a new one if there's already an unnamed one.
sections.tip: A new group carries 'isNew' + 'isDirty' flags; if you leave the page without saving, it's lost.

sections.heading: Group Card — Drag Handle
sections.body: The drag handle on the left. Drag to reorder groups; reordering automatically marks them dirty.

sections.heading: Group Card — Name
sections.body: Required group title. Customer sees this name. Empty title = can't save.

sections.heading: Group Card — Min / Max Selection
sections.body:
Min: minimum picks the customer must make.
Max: maximum picks allowed.

Combinations:
• Min=0, Max=1 → optional single pick
• Min=1, Max=1 → required single pick (radio-style)
• Min=0, Max=N → multiple optional
• Min=1, Max=N → required multiple
sections.example:
Doneness: Min=1, Max=1 (required single)
Extra Toppings: Min=0, Max=5 (optional, up to 5)

sections.heading: Group Card — Free Tagging
sections.body: Checkbox next to the tag group. When on, the customer gets a free-form 'Other / Note' field.
sections.tip: Turn OFF for groups with strict categories (e.g. 'Meat Pizza Choice'); free text complicates kitchen workflow.

sections.heading: Group Card — Save This Group
sections.body: If you've changed only one group, a RED 'Save' button appears just for it. Sends only that group.
sections.tip: If two groups have changes, you can save one before the other when one is more urgent.

sections.heading: Group Card — Delete / Cancel
sections.body:
Newly added (unsaved) groups show an 'X' (cancel) — just removes from the page, doesn't go to backend.

Saved groups show a Trash icon — confirmation popup, then deletion from backend.

sections.heading: Group Card — Collapse / Expand
sections.body: Saved groups have an up/down arrow instead of pencil. Hides/shows group content; useful when you have many groups for visual clarity.

sections.heading: Tab: Options
sections.body:
Individual choices inside a group. For each: name, price, default flag, mandatory flag, min count, max count (portion-style).

'+ Add Option' adds a new option; drag-and-drop reorders.

sections.heading: Tab: Related Products
sections.body:
Which products this group shows for. Three dropdowns chain: category → product → portion.

• 'All Categories' → all products
• Pick a category → all products in that category
• Pick a product too → only that product
• Pick a portion too → only that portion of that product
sections.tip: Dropdowns are now SEARCHABLE — type to filter long lists.

sections.heading: Related Products — + New Relation
sections.body:
A group can have multiple relations. E.g. 'Doneness' can bind to both Burger and Steak categories.
'+ Add Relation' opens a new row.

sections.heading: No-Relations Warning
sections.body: A group with no relations turns red and shows 'This group isn't assigned to any product'; it can't be shown to customers. Add at least one relation before saving.

qrThemes.title: QR Menu Themes — Help
qrThemes.intro: Pick the menu design customers see on their PHONE. Colour palette, category layout, product card design — all driven by the theme. 5 ready-made themes; selection saves instantly.

sections.heading: Theme Cards (Left Panel)
sections.body:
5 theme cards stacked. Each shows:
• Theme colour dot (visual hint, far left)
• Theme name (Theme 1, Theme 2, ...)
• Tag (Classic, Modern, Natural, Sunny, Fresh)

The currently active card has an indigo border and ✓ mark.

sections.heading: Theme Selection (Auto-Save)
sections.body: Clicking a theme card immediately dispatches a save to the backend. While saving, a small spinner spins on that theme card. Once saved, the right-side iframe refreshes with the new theme.
sections.warning: Saved themes go LIVE on the customer menu instantly. Any customer browsing right now sees the new design.

sections.heading: Device Selector (iPhone / Android / Tablet)
sections.body:
Three toggles in the right panel. Resizes the preview frame to match the picked device:
• iPhone: narrow vertical frame
• Android: similar but a different frame
• Tablet: wider frame

Use it to check how the menu looks on the device your customers actually use.

sections.heading: Live Preview (iframe)
sections.body: The right panel shows your REAL menu in a frame, served from your real tenant URL. NOT A MOCK — actual menu, actual products, actual prices.
sections.tip: The phone frame stays put; only the content (iframe) reloads. No flicker when switching themes.

sections.heading: Refresh Button (RefreshCcw)
sections.body: Small refresh button above the preview. Cache-busts and reloads the iframe. Press it if the theme changed but the preview didn't update.

sections.heading: Live URL Link
sections.body: Below the hero or near the iframe, your tenant URL (e.g. liwacafe.liwamenu.com) is a clickable link. Opens the menu at full size in a new tab.
sections.example: https://<tenant>.liwamenu.com — the actual address your customers visit

sections.heading: Theme Tag Cheat-Sheet
sections.body:
Each theme has a personality:
• Classic (orange) — warm, traditional cafés
• Modern (purple) — minimalist, next-gen restaurants
• Natural (green) — organic, vegan, seafood
• Sunny (yellow) — fun, fast-food
• Fresh (deep green) — healthy, salad bar

The right theme makes customers 'feel' your restaurant correctly.

sections.heading: No Save Button — Auto!
sections.body: There's NO manual save button; clicking a theme card IS the save. If something fails a toast appears; even if you close the page, the last picked theme persists.
sections.warning: If you accidentally pick the wrong theme, just click the right one — your old theme isn't lost instantly, but the new one does save.

tvThemes.title: TV Menu Themes — Help
tvThemes.intro: Themes designed for a BIG SCREEN inside your restaurant. Counter-service cafés, fast-food, or self-service spots that mirror their menu onto a TV. Independent from the QR menu — separate theme picker.

sections.heading: What is a TV Menu?
sections.body: Hang a TV in your restaurant, plug an Android TV box / mini PC behind it, browse to your tenant URL — your menu fills the screen. Customers at the counter read the menu off the TV.
sections.example: Migros in-store cafés, Starbucks, fast-food chains use this approach.

sections.heading: Independent of QR Theme
sections.body: TV theme and QR theme save SEPARATELY. The customer's phone and the wall TV can have different designs; that's natural — what's tiny on a phone should be big cards on a TV.
sections.tip: 'Modern' on phone (info-dense) + 'Classic' on TV (big cards) is a great combo.

sections.heading: Theme Cards
sections.body:
Same 5 ready-made designs as QR themes. Colours and vibes differ; layout is optimised for the TV screen.

Each card shows:
• Colour dot (visual hint)
• Theme name
• Tag (Classic / Modern / Natural / Sunny / Fresh)

sections.heading: Theme Selection (Auto-Save)
sections.body: Click a card and the system saves immediately. The loading spinner spins on the card; once saved, the right-side TV preview refreshes.
sections.warning: If your in-restaurant TV is on, the theme change appears on it instantly. Switching themes while customers are present can cause confusion.

sections.heading: Device Selector
sections.body: On the right panel you can change the preview device. For TV, the most natural choice is 'Tablet' or a wide frame.

sections.heading: Live Preview
sections.body: The right panel iframes your real tenant URL in a frame. Theme changes reflect instantly.
sections.tip: The Refresh (RefreshCcw) button cache-busts the preview.

sections.heading: Live URL Link
sections.body: Next to the preview, your tenant URL is a clickable link. Opens the menu at full size in a new tab — useful to check 'how does it look fullscreen' before plugging into the TV.

qrCode.title: Generate QR Code — Help
qrCode.intro: Bulk-generate the QR codes that go on your restaurant tables. Each QR sends scanners to your tenant URL with a ?tableNumber=… parameter, so the system knows which table when an order is placed.

sections.heading: Left Panel — Technical Parameters
sections.body: The top section of the left panel: table-naming rules (prefix, suffix, start, end). The number of QRs to generate is determined by these fields; the 'X QR' counter at the top of the page reflects them.

sections.heading: Table Prefix
sections.body: Text prepended to each QR's table identifier. Can be empty.

sections.heading: Table Suffix
sections.body: Text appended to the table identifier. Can be empty.

sections.heading: Start Number
sections.body: Range start. Can be 1, 5, 100 — anything.
sections.example: If your first table number is 11 instead of 1: Start: 11

sections.heading: End Number
sections.body: Range end. One QR is generated for EVERY integer between start and end inclusive.

sections.heading: Empty Range = Single QR
sections.body: If Start AND End are both empty, only ONE QR is generated; it contains 'prefix + suffix'. No numeric digit is appended.
sections.example:
Prefix: 'TAKEOUT', range empty → one QR with 'TAKEOUT'
Prefix: 'VIP', Suffix: '-1' → one QR with 'VIP-1'
sections.tip: Want a special 'Takeaway' QR for the counter? Leave the range empty and enter 'TAKEOUT' as the prefix.

sections.heading: Visual — Gradient Start Colour
sections.body: The QR's primary colour (starts top-left). Pick via colour picker or type a HEX. Black-black = classic QR.

sections.heading: Visual — Gradient End Colour
sections.body: The QR's second colour (ends bottom-right). Different start/end = gradient.

sections.heading: Display Center Logo
sections.body:
Toggle. ON → an image/logo shows in the QR's centre. OFF → the centre stays empty (the dot pattern continues).

When OFF, the logo upload area below is hidden.

sections.heading: Brand Logo (Upload Area)
sections.body:
Appears when the toggle is ON. Upload PNG/JPG. Transparent PNG looks cleanest.

If you don't upload AND you've set a sequential range, the system AUTOMATICALLY places the table number in the centre of each QR (white circle, black digit).
sections.example:
No logo + range 1-30 → each QR shows 1, 2, 3... in the centre.
With logo → each QR shows your logo in the centre.
sections.tip: Want both logo AND table number? That's not supported on this page; you'd need to edit the generated PNG.

sections.heading: Preview — Default QR
sections.body:
The top of the left panel. Live preview of the QR for your tenant root URL (https://<tenant>.liwamenu.com — no tableNumber).

Updates instantly as settings (colour, logo) change.

sections.heading: Preview — Download PNG
sections.body: Indigo button below the preview. Downloads only the default QR (tenant root) as a PNG. Useful for 'come pick up' / 'takeout' QRs.

sections.heading: Preview — Tenant URL Link
sections.body: Below the download button, your tenant URL is a clickable link. Opens the menu in a new tab.

sections.heading: Hero — Bulk Counter
sections.body: The hero subtitle shows 'X QR'. Auto-calculated from the range + prefix/suffix in the left panel. Empty everything = 0.

sections.heading: Hero — Download All QR Codes
sections.body: After clicking 'Generate Batch' and producing QRs, this download button appears in the hero. Bundles every generated QR into one ZIP.
sections.example: After generating 30 QRs → one click → qr-codes.zip

sections.heading: Hero — Clear All
sections.body: Removes every generated QR (only from the view; backend isn't affected). Useful before regenerating.

sections.heading: Right Panel — Generated QRs
sections.body:
Empty state (QR icon + 'Start Generating' button) until you generate. After generation: a 4-column grid:
• Each card shows the QR image
• Hovering reveals a 'Download' button
• Underneath: the table label (e.g. TABLE-1)

sections.heading: Right Panel — Generate Batch (Output Header)
sections.body: After QRs are generated, a 'Generate Batch' button appears in the output panel header. Use it to RE-generate with the same parameters (tweak a colour and regenerate, etc.).

sections.heading: Single QR Download
sections.body:
Hovering a generated QR card reveals an indigo 'Download' button. Downloads that one QR as PNG.

Filename is 'qr-table-TABLE-1.png' format — Turkish characters get ASCII-folded (e.g. 'Bahçe-1' → 'Bahce-1').

sections.heading: Generate Batch — License Check
sections.body: First thing the button does: checks whether your restaurant has a QR license. None → red toast 'Restaurant license not found'. Inactive → 'License inactive'.
sections.warning: Generating QRs needs a license; without one, bulk generation won't run.

sections.heading: Nothing Filled In
sections.body: If prefix, suffix, AND range are ALL empty, Generate Batch shows a red toast: 'Please enter a table prefix, suffix, or number range'. Won't let you generate empty QRs.
